covered_sentences: When Edgar Linton arrived, Cathy told me to leave the room.
I was polishing the silver.
"You can go now, Nelly," she said, in that imperious way of hers.
Now the master had told me to stay with them whenever Edgar Linton came to visit, so I replied, "I will just finish polishing the silver."
Miss Cathy, I'm sure Mr. Linton does not mind.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Three-panel horizontal sequence, ratio 2:1:1 (left panel largest — the main confrontation; center and right panels smaller, equal width). Left panel: camera positioned at the right side of the Thrushcross Grange drawing room, angled 30 degrees left-of-front, medium shot from approximately adult chest height; Catherine stands in the foreground-left area facing the housekeeper; the housekeeper is in the center with silver polishing cloth in hand; Edgar is visible in the right background near the doorway, recently arrived. Center panel: camera cuts to a medium-close shot of Catherine from slightly below, angled 15 degrees right-of-front, cutting from chest upward; her imperious expression fills the frame; the gold-framed portrait and fireplace edge are barely visible in the background. Right panel: camera at medium-close range of the housekeeper, angled 20 degrees left-of-front, cutting from chest upward; her expression is calm but firm; the silver polishing cloth is visible in her hands. Per-panel budget: left 3 recognizable (Catherine + housekeeper + Edgar), center 1 recognizable (Catherine), right 1 recognizable (housekeeper).

Left panel: the housekeeper stands in the center of the drawing room, silver polishing cloth in her right hand and a silver candlestick or tray in the other, engaged in polishing; Catherine approaches from the left with an imperious posture, one hand gesturing toward the door; Edgar stands near the right background doorway, hat in hand, expression slightly uncertain. Center panel: Catherine's face and upper body fill the frame, chin raised, expression imperious and commanding; her speech bubble contains the words "go now" (direct text extract, compressed to two words from the original). Right panel: the housekeeper's face and upper body fill the frame, expression calm and quietly resolute, silver cloth still visible in her hands; her speech bubble contains the words "finish polishing" (direct text extract).

Thrushcross Grange drawing room, daytime; left panel: deep red carpet underfoot, ornate marble fireplace with the oval gold-framed portrait above it in the background right; high windows with heavy curtains on the left; the housekeeper stands on the carpet approximately two body-lengths from the fireplace; Edgar at the doorway in the right background — door frame clearly above his head; Catherine walking toward the housekeeper, her head top at approximately two-thirds adult height. Center and right panels: only fireplace edge and curtain edge visible as background fragments consistent with the drawing room.

Bright daytime drawing room light through the high windows; warm cream-gold palette; center panel slightly warmer spotlight effect on Catherine's imperious face; right panel slightly cooler and calmer.

Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic period): dark brown mid-length neatly arranged hair, slender figure, light cream fine dress with delicate pleats reaching ankle; in left panel approaching the housekeeper — her head at approximately two-thirds the height of the door frame visible behind Edgar; in center panel her face fills the frame from chest up. Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round face with calm firm expression, dark domestic dress with white apron, tied collar, silver polishing cloth in hand; in left panel standing in the room center — her head top at approximately the same height as Catherine's or slightly taller due to Catherine's youth; in right panel her face fills the frame from chest up. Edgar Linton: light golden long curly hair, large calm light-blue eyes, refined face, light-colored gentleman coat; standing near the doorway in left panel background — door frame clearly above his head, his figure smaller in the background. Proportion anchor: in left panel Catherine's head at approximately two-thirds of door frame height; housekeeper's head top approximately level with Catherine's; Edgar's head below door frame upper edge in the background.

covered_sentences: She came up to me, and supposing that Edgar could not see her, she pinched my arm.
It really hurt a lot, and I was determined that Mr. Linton should notice it.
"Oh, miss," I cried, "why did you pinch me?"
"I didn't touch you. You lying creature," said Catherine, and she tried to pinch me again, her face red with rage.
Yes, you did, miss. Here. And I showed the purple mark on my arm so that Mr. Linton could see it.
She slapped my face so hard that my eyes filled with water.

covered_sentences: "Catherine, love, Catherine," cried Linton, shocked by her behavior.
"Leave the room," said Catherine, trembling all over.
Little Hareton, who followed me everywhere, was in the room, and when he saw her slap me, he began to cry.
Now she grabbed his shoulders and shook him violently.
I tried to free the child from her grasp, so she slapped him across the cheek as well.
I took little Hareton and went off to the kitchen.

Three-panel horizontal sequence, ratio 2:1:1 (left panel largest — the key violent action cluster; center and right smaller panels for Edgar's shocked reaction and the exit). covered_sentences: But I left the door open so that I could see what happened.
Edgar turned pale and picked up his hat.
I thought, "That's right, you've seen her true character now. Take warning and go."
"Where are you going?" asked Catherine. "You must not go."
"I must and shall," said Edgar.
Catherine dropped onto her knees by a chair and burst into tears as Edgar left the sitting room.
